citations.description: Ticket status is in_progress; comments contain only the FORCE_CLAIM note, no publish confirmation.

findings.detail: This criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable). The codebase contains version = "0.10.0" in pyproject.toml and a release.yml workflow capable of publishing, but actual PyPI publication is an external outcome invisible in the repository. No attestation (workflow run URL, PyPI release URL, or observed outcome) is recorded in the ticket comments confirming a successful dispatch and publish.
findings.remediation: Dispatch release.yml with version=0.10.0 from main, approve the pypi environment deployment, then record proof as a ticket comment including the GitHub Actions run URL and the PyPI release URL (https://pypi.org/project/nava-rebar/0.10.0/).

citations.description: Ticket comments contain no GitHub Release URL, MCP Registry confirmation, or Homebrew bump evidence.

findings.criterion: GitHub Release v0.10.0 created (Latest, artifacts attached); MCP Registry shows 0.10.0; Homebrew formula bumped.
findings.detail: This compound criterion is untagged (codebase-verifiable). The release.yml workflow has jobs for github_release and mcp_registry, and server.json is bumped to 0.10.0, but none of these downstream operational steps can be confirmed in the repository. The ticket contains no attestation (GitHub Release URL, MCP Registry confirmation, Homebrew formula PR/commit) that these channels were updated.
findings.remediation: After a successful release.yml run, record as a ticket comment: (1) the GitHub Release URL (https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/releases/tag/v0.10.0), (2) MCP Registry confirmation (e.g., registry listing URL or API response showing 0.10.0), and (3) the Homebrew formula autobump PR or commit URL.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
